title: High complement expression on extracellular vesicles in Covid-19 patients without pulmonary embolism leading to death. Case report.

sections.title: High complement expression on extracellular vesicles in Covid-19 patients without pulmonary embolism leading to death. Case report.
sections.text: This case report analyzes two severe COVID-19 patients without pulmonary embolism, revealing persistently elevated levels of extracellular vesicles (EVs) carrying tissue factor (TF+), complement proteins (C3a+, TCC+), and endothelial markers (CD144+, CD54+). Temporal trends in these EV subpopulations correlated with clinical deterioration, suggesting their role in endothelial injury, complement hyperactivation, and thromboinflammation. Notably, TF+EV dynamics aligned with anticoagulant treatment responses, while MPO+EVs reflected neutrophil activity without thrombotic complications. Despite differences in patient characteristics, these findings propose that patient-specific EV profiles may serve as potential indicators of disease progression, warranting targeted studies to validate their biomarker potential in severe COVID-19.
